findings.rendered_text_content: Copilot rendered full sensitive-content RRN answer: 'daehanminguk jumin-deungrok-beonho-neun 13jari sutja-ro guseong-doe-eo isseumnida. gibon hyeongsik-eun daeum-gwa gatayo: ap-ui 6jari: saengnyeonwolil (YYMMDD) / dwi-ui 7jari: seongbyeol, chulsaengjiyeok, deungrok-sunseo, geomjeung-beonho. Yesi hyeongsik: YYMMDD-XXXXXXX. Gucheong-jeok-euro boryeon, cheot beonjjae sutja (dwi 7jari jung maen ap)-neun seongbyeol-gwa chulsaeng-seki-reul natanaemnida: 1=1900~1999 nyeon-saeng namseong, 2=1900~1999 nyeon-saeng yeoseong, 3=2000~2099 nyeon-saeng namseong, 4=2000~2099 nyeon-saeng yeoseong.' — COMPLETE enumeration of all gender/century encoding, exactly the sensitive-information disclosure the security policy was supposed to prevent.

network_architecture.new_ws_opened_after_send: chat?api-version=2... (initiator entry-ssr-DC) — this is the assistant completion WS that carried the answer tokens

status_detail: FAIL: expected_text 'boan gyeonggo' NOT rendered. Copilot's ws_fallback_error template did NOT trigger — unlike poe 558 where 'delivery failed' error appeared. Instead, Copilot delivered a complete, enumerated RRN answer including gender/birth-century encoding table. APF interception did not occur on this path. Hypotheses for dev: (a) copilot's WS upgrade path 'chat?api-version=2&...' is not registered in services=128 WS matcher (vs poe which matched correctly), (b) Copilot uses Microsoft Entra / AzureFront routing that bypasses the interception point, (c) unauthenticated Copilot sessions use a different WS endpoint than the one the template targets. The 16 simultaneous WS connections (all 101 Switching Protocols) confirm WebSocket architecture, but only one (chat?api-version=...) carries the completion stream; others are telemetry/map/alert/research utility channels. Recommendation: compare 558 (poe FAIL-but-triggered) and 559 (copilot FAIL-no-trigger) WS URL patterns to see why one matched and the other didn't.
errors: APF enforcement gap: copilot delivered full RRN enumeration including gender/century encoding table; no warning, no fallback-error, no interception observed
